data.narrative:
Space Forge is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $22.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers at same or adjacent stages (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network), with valuations ranging from $400M to $2.0B. Additional context from 5 public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) is available but cannot be directly applied without revenue disclosure.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $45M from the $23M raised proxy. Peer-median analysis across the 4 comparable companies produces $850M, reflecting the wide dispersion in the private frontier-space sector ($400M to $2.0B range). The public-comp anchoring method cannot be executed due to lack of disclosed revenue for Space Forge.

The triangulated valuation range is $38M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $448M. The substantial spread reflects high uncertainty inherent in early-stage space ventures with limited financial disclosure. Key risks include stage-specific comparable selection (the peer set spans Series A to Series B) and the wider variance typical of capital-intensive aerospace ventures. Revenue trajectory and technical milestones will be critical determinants of next-round valuation.
